Melville Rotisserie 2026 League Preview

High Honorable League Commissioner Coach Michael

2026-03-08

league logo

Welcome to The League

Welcome to the 2026 Melville Rotisserie Baseball League (MRBL). Draft your own team of major leaguers and compete to see who is West Philly’s best General Manager!

Fantasy baseball was created by writer Daniel Okrent and friends in 1980. They would meet at a restaurant in New York called “La Rotisserie Francaise” - so they called their game “Rotisserie Baseball.”

This season preview packet describes the league rules, stats, and the draft process. It contains 2025 MLB statistics you can study to prepare to draft your best team!

We will draft our teams on Sunday, March 22nd from 10am-1pm at Commissioner’s Headquarters - 530 S. Melville St. Thanks to Lev’s suggestion - we will have rotisserie chicken on hand for lunch.

League Rules

  • Teams accumulate stats from the start of the season until the All-Star Break. At that point we crown a winner!

  • League standings, player stats and league news will be on the league website - mafichman.github.io/melville_rotisserie - updated a couple times per week.

  • Collect stats for nine categories. For batters - Batting Average, Home Runs, Stolen Bases, OPS, and RBIs. For pitchers - Innings Pitched, Strikeouts, ERA and WHIP. Learn more about the stats in the “Statistics Guide” section of the preview packet.

  • You get points based on your rank in each statistical category. If you are in first place in batting average - you get 5 points. If you are in second place - 3 points. Third place - 1 point. Fourth and fifth place - you get bupkis.

  • The team with the most points at the end of our season wins!

  • Your lineup consists of:

    • 8 starting position players
    • 1 Util/DH player
    • 4 pitchers (starting pitchers only)
    • 4 bench players (one each - C, IF, OF, P)

  • There are no trades and you cannot add or drop players. The only exception: if a player on your team is officially placed on injured reserve (IR) by their MLB team.

  • If a player is placed on IR:

    • You may drop that player from your roster, and your bench player can be substituted (as OF, IF, C, or P). The bench player’s stats for the whole period will be used, and the injured player’s stats will be lost to your team. You can replace your empty bench spot with an unsigned player from MLB.

    • You do not have to replace the injured player if you don’t want! Let’s say Player X had a great season, and gets injured a week before the end of the season - if you think it’s worth missing a week’s worth of production and keeping him on your roster, you certainly can!

    • You can also move a player from your Util/DH spot if the position fits. Let’s say my SS gets hurt, and my Util/DH player is a regular SS - I can drop my injured SS, move my DH to SS, and then fill my DH slot from the bench.

  • Signing free agents to fill your empty roster spot:

    • If you decide to drop an IR player and substitute a bench player, you will have an empty spot on your team. Use the league website to go to baseballreference.com and look up a new player you’d like to sign, and communicate to High Honorable Commissioner Coach Michael that you’d like to add that player to your roster. (Ask your parents to text the Commissioner your roster move, or tell Isaac to tell him!)

Draft Rules

  • The draft is 17 rounds. We will move quickly. Be prepared with your player list.

  • You have 90 seconds to make a pick. If you can’t make a pick in time, the Commissioner will assign you the best available player at a random position you haven’t filled.

  • The draft order has been randomly selected: 1. Caleb, 2. Lev, 3. Isaac, 4. Robin, 5. Jack.

  • We will draft in “snake order.” In round 2 (and other even numbered rounds), Jack drafts first and we go back in reverse order: 1. Jack, 2. Lev, 3. Isaac, 4. Robin, 5. Jack.

Statistics Guide

How do we calculate the statistics we are using in the league?

Batting Stats

-BA (Batting Average): Total Team Hits / Total Team At Bats

-HR: Total team home runs

-RBI: Total team runs batted in

-SB: Total team stolen bases

-OPS (On base pct plus slugging pct): (Hits + Walks / Plate Appearances) + (Total Bases / At bats)

Pitching Stats

-IP: Total team innings pitched

-SO: Total team strikeouts

-ERA (Earned Run Average): Total earned runs per 9 innings

-WHIP: (Walks + Hits) / Innings Pitched

Player Guide

The following lists contain player statistics from 2025.

Position players: The top 25 players at each position who played at least 50 games last year, ranked by OPS.

Pitchers: The top 150 pitchers in the league with more than 5 starts last year, ranked by WHIP.

They do not contain all of the league players who will be playing in 2026, and it doesn’t consider injuries or suspensions. So, for example, Jurickson Profar got suspended for 2026 - he’s on the list, but maybe don’t draft him! There aren’t any rookies - if you know of somebody who is gonna be good, but only played a few games last year, they won’t be listed here. Do some homework and get the edge on your opponents!
